title: Ecolier Practice Set 13 - Numbers and Geometry
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official competition questions.

questions.question_text: In the number 4,731, what digit is in the hundreds place?
questions.solution: The digits of 4,731 are: 4 (thousands), 7 (hundreds), 3 (tens), 1 (ones). The hundreds digit is 7.

questions.question_text: What is 467 rounded to the nearest ten?
questions.solution: The ones digit of 467 is 7, which is 5 or more, so we round up. 467 rounds to 470.

questions.question_text: Which type of angle is shown in the figure?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='80' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='20' y2='20' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='20' y='70' width='10' height='10' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: Two line segments meeting at a corner with a small square marking the angle.
questions.solution: The small square symbol at the vertex indicates a right angle of 90 degrees.

questions.question_text: Which of the following numbers is a multiple of 6?
questions.solution: Multiples of 6 are 6, 12, 18, 24, 30, 36, 42. So 42 is a multiple of 6, since 42 = 6 x 7.

questions.question_text: How many lines of symmetry does the rectangle shown have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='20' width='90' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.solution: A non-square rectangle has exactly 2 lines of symmetry: one vertical through the midpoint and one horizontal through the midpoint.

questions.question_text: On the grid, what are the coordinates of the marked point P?
questions.image_description: A coordinate grid with x and y axes marked in units of 1, and point P plotted.
questions.solution: Point P is 3 units right of the origin along the x-axis and 2 units up along the y-axis, giving coordinates (3, 2).

questions.question_text: Which number is a factor of 15?
questions.solution: The factors of 15 are 1, 3, 5, and 15. From the options, only 5 is a factor of 15.

questions.question_text: Which quadrilateral has exactly one pair of parallel sides?
questions.solution: A trapezoid has exactly one pair of parallel sides. Rectangles, rhombi, and parallelograms have two pairs, and kites have none.

questions.question_text: Which number has a 6 in the thousands place and a 4 in the tens place?
questions.solution: In 6,249 the thousands digit is 6, hundreds is 2, tens is 4, and ones is 9. Both conditions are met.

questions.question_text: Lina rounded a number to the nearest hundred and got 800. Which of these could be her original number?
questions.solution: To round to 800 (nearest hundred) the number must lie between 750 and 849. Only 846 is in that range.

questions.question_text: An angle measures 115 degrees. What type of angle is it?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='100' y2='80' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='60' y2='20' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M 45 80 A 25 25 0 0 0 38 60' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: An angle with a curved arc marking its measure.
questions.solution: An obtuse angle is greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees. 115 degrees fits this range, so it is obtuse.

questions.question_text: What is the least common multiple of 4 and 6?
questions.solution: Multiples of 4 are 4, 8, 12, 16. Multiples of 6 are 6, 12, 18. The smallest shared multiple is 12.

questions.question_text: The figure shows a capital letter H. How many lines of symmetry does it have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='15' width='12' height='70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='53' y='15' width='12' height='70' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='15' y='44' width='50' height='12' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/></svg>
questions.image_description: The capital letter H drawn from rectangles.
questions.solution: The letter H has a vertical line of symmetry and a horizontal line of symmetry, so 2 in total.

questions.question_text: Starting at point (2, 1), you move 3 units up and 2 units right. What are the new coordinates?
questions.solution: Moving 2 units right changes x from 2 to 4. Moving 3 units up changes y from 1 to 4. The new point is (4, 4).

questions.question_text: How many factors does the number 16 have?
questions.solution: The factors of 16 are 1, 2, 4, 8, and 16. That is 5 factors in total.

questions.question_text: A quadrilateral has 4 equal sides but no right angles. What is it called?
questions.image_description: A rhombus drawn as a diamond with four equal sides.
questions.solution: A rhombus has 4 equal sides. With right angles it would be a special case (a square), but without them it is simply a rhombus.

questions.question_text: Using the digits 3, 5, 7, and 9 each exactly once, what is the largest 4-digit number you can make?
questions.solution: Place the largest digits in the highest place values: 9 thousands, 7 hundreds, 5 tens, 3 ones. The number is 9,753.

questions.question_text: When rounded to the nearest ten, which of these numbers does NOT round to 350?
questions.solution: Numbers that round to 350 (nearest ten) are from 345 through 354. The number 356 rounds to 360.

questions.question_text: Two angles together form a straight line. One angle measures 65 degrees. What is the measure of the other angle?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><line x1='10' y1='60' x2='150' y2='60' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='80' y1='60' x2='130' y2='15' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M 100 60 A 20 20 0 0 0 96 46' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A straight line with a ray splitting it into two angles.
questions.solution: Angles on a straight line add up to 180 degrees, so the other angle is 180 - 65 = 115 degrees.

questions.question_text: How many numbers between 1 and 50 (inclusive) are multiples of both 3 and 5?
questions.solution: Multiples of both 3 and 5 are multiples of 15. From 1 to 50 these are 15, 30, and 45, so 3 numbers.

questions.question_text: The figure is made by shading squares on a 4 by 4 grid. How many lines of symmetry does the shaded figure have?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1' fill='none'><rect x='20' y='20' width='80' height='80'/><line x1='40' y1='20' x2='40' y2='100'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='60' y2='100'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='100'/><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='100' y2='40'/><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='100' y2='60'/><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='100' y2='80'/></g><rect x='40' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='60' y='20' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='20' y='40' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='80' y='40' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='20' y='60' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='80' y='60' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='40' y='80' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/><rect x='60' y='80' width='20' height='20' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A 4 by 4 grid with 8 shaded cells forming a symmetric ring-like pattern.
questions.solution: The shaded pattern is symmetric about the vertical centerline, the horizontal centerline, and both diagonals. That gives 4 lines of symmetry.

questions.question_text: Points A at (1, 1), B at (5, 1), and C at (5, 4) are three corners of a rectangle. What are the coordinates of the fourth corner D?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' fill='none'><g stroke='#cccccc' stroke-width='1'><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='20' y2='120'/><line x1='40' y1='20' x2='40' y2='120'/><line x1='60' y1='20' x2='60' y2='120'/><line x1='80' y1='20' x2='80' y2='120'/><line x1='100' y1='20' x2='100' y2='120'/><line x1='120' y1='20' x2='120' y2='120'/><line x1='20' y1='20' x2='120' y2='20'/><line x1='20' y1='40' x2='120' y2='40'/><line x1='20' y1='60' x2='120' y2='60'/><line x1='20' y1='80' x2='120' y2='80'/><line x1='20' y1='100' x2='120' y2='100'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='120' y2='120'/></g><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='130' y2='120' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='20' y1='120' x2='20' y2='10' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='40' cy='100' r='3' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='30' y='95' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>A</text><circle cx='120' cy='100' r='3' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='124' y='95' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>B</text><circle cx='120' cy='40' r='3' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor'/><text x='124' y='36' font-size='10' fill='currentColor'>C</text></svg>
questions.image_description: A coordinate grid showing three points A, B, and C marked as three corners of a rectangle.
questions.solution: For a rectangle with corners A(1,1), B(5,1), and C(5,4), the fourth corner D shares the x-coordinate of A and the y-coordinate of C. So D = (1, 4).

questions.question_text: Which of the following numbers has exactly 3 factors?
questions.solution: The factors of 9 are 1, 3, and 9, which is exactly 3. The others (6, 8, 10, 14) each have 4 factors.

questions.question_text: A quadrilateral has two pairs of parallel sides and four right angles, but the sides are not all equal in length. What is it?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='15' y='20' width='110' height='40' fill='#f0f0f0' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'/><rect x='15' y='20' width='8' height='8' fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='1'/></svg>
questions.image_description: A rectangle with a right-angle marker at one corner.
questions.solution: Two pairs of parallel sides with four right angles describes a rectangle. Since the sides are not all equal it is not a square.
